steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes how the two works are similar. Any choice that contrasts the works or highlights only one of them fails. The notes show both works use acrylic paint — that is the shared trait.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Artist Jean-Michel Basquiat completed *Untitled (Two Heads on Gold)* in 1982, and *Jazz* followed in 1986.: Lists completion dates in sequence; this is chronological ordering, not a similarity between the works. ✗
  • Choice B — Between artist Jean-Michel Basquiat's works *Untitled (Two Heads on Gold)* and *Jazz*, the former is the larger of the two.: Explicitly compares their sizes — a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice C — Composed by artist Jean-Michel Basquiat in 1982 and 1986, respectively, both *Untitled (Two Heads on Gold)* and *Jazz* use acrylic paint as a medium.: States directly that both works share acrylic paint as a medium — a genuine similarity supported by the notes. ✓
  • Choice D — Artist Jean-Michel Basquiat's work *Untitled (Two Heads on Gold)* is composed of acrylic and oil stick, while *Jazz* is composed of acrylic, oil, graphite, crayon, and paper collage.: Uses "while" to contrast the full composition lists; this highlights differences rather than similarity. ✗
That matches Choice C — Composed by artist Jean-Michel Basquiat in 1982 and 1986, respectively, both *Untitled (Two Heads on Gold)* and *Jazz* use acrylic paint as a medium..
